reason: Both are correct, but B is too thin — it hits data loop, workflow embedding, and switching costs in one sentence but never addresses distribution/incumbent advantages or the 'speed of iteration beats racing the labs' point, and doesn't explicitly debunk model quality as a moat. A covers all four key points (workflow depth, data feedback loops, distribution/trust, and explicitly states model quality was never durable) plus adds useful, non-padded specifics (evals/reliability, cost/latency engineering) and a concrete pivot recommendation. A's added length is substantive, not filler, so it's more decision-relevant despite being longer.
